fields.icd_description: Fracture of neck of right femur, initial encounter
fields.icd_conflict_detail: No conflict found. All three documents (referral form, clinical notes, and prescription) consistently list ICD-10 code S72.001A with the description 'Fracture of neck of right femur, initial encounter'.
fields.priority_reason: Patient is a post-surgical case with an acute femoral neck fracture (S72.001A), representing a high-severity orthopedic injury with active rehabilitation protocol requiring immediate mobility aid support.
fields.notes: 1) Physician NPI is blank across all three documents — follow up with Desert Orthopedics at (760) 555-0211 to obtain NPI before billing. 2) Patient height is 6'3" and weight is only 137 lbs, which is notably low for that height — verify patient anthropometrics to ensure correct forearm crutch sizing and that standard weight-bearing capacity is appropriate. 3) No preferred appointment window was specified — confirm delivery scheduling with adjuster Sarah Mitchell. 4) Prescription is valid until 07/21/2026 (90 days from 04/22/2026). 5) The adjuster name (Sarah Mitchell) matches the case manager name on the referral submission footer, confirming single point of contact.

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Dear Ms. Mitchell,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding an active durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured, **Gary Mitchell**, under workers' compensation claim **WC-2026-084463**.

In order to process and route this referral, we require the following information, which is currently missing from the submitted documentation:

1. **Appointment Window** *(Hard Block — routing is on hold pending this item)*
2. **Preferred Language**
3. **Ordering Physician NPI**

Please note that the missing appointment window is currently preventing us from routing this referral entirely. As such, we kindly ask that you provide all outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this email to avoid any further delay in care for the patient.

You may reply directly to this email or contact our intake team through your preferred method on file.

We appreciate your prompt attention to this matter and look forward to your response.

Sincerely,

**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME | Intake Department
